business.description:
Trade Desk (TTD) shares moved higher after reports of potential easing in US Iran tensions, as investors rotated back into software as a service names following an earlier sector correction.
See our latest analysis for Trade Desk.
Even after today’s relief rally, Trade Desk’s recent moves sit against a weak backdrop, with a 30 day share price return of 11.8% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 55.1%, suggesting recent momentum is more rebound than trend change.

business.title: Barrick Weighs Reko Diq Delay Against Record Results And Portfolio Risk
business.description:
Barrick Mining (NYSE:B) has delayed development of its Reko Diq copper gold project in Pakistan, citing heightened regional security risks.
The project review has been extended until at least mid 2027, affecting a key growth asset for the company.
The announcement came alongside record quarterly results and the launch of an operational review by the interim CEO.
For investors watching NYSE:B, Reko Diq is positioned as a cornerstone copper and gold project in a sector where large scale...

business.description:
AMP (ASX:AMP) has drawn fresh investor attention after its Board approved a share buyback of up to 253,984 shares for A$150 million. The program will run through 31 December 2026.
See our latest analysis for AMP.
The buyback and recent CFO transition come after a 5.33% 1 month share price return but a 29.78% share price decline over the past quarter, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 11.37% shows income and reinvested dividends telling a more supportive story.

science.title: Southport training roots highlighted as Canadian astronaut embarks on Artemis II mission
science.description: A local connection in Southport is reaching beyond Earth’s orbit as Canadian Space Agency astronaut Col. Jeremy Hansen takes part in NASA’s Artemis II mission, with ties to Southport Aerospace Centre near Portage la Prairie underscoring the region’s role in shaping world-class aircrew. Related stories: Southport's 3 CFFTS: Shaping Canada's future Air Force pilots VIDEO: A tour of Southport’s KF Aerospace training complex NASA’s Artemis II mission successfully launched on April 1, 2026, at 6:35 p.m. EDT from Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The historic crewed lunar flyby marks the first time humans have travelled beyond low Earth orbit since 1972, and the mission is currently underway with a planned duration of approximately 10 days. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/pol-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1647631135332-bbm1'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1647631135332]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'southport-training-roots-highlighted-as-canadian-astronaut-embarks-on-artemis-ii-mission'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); Hansen’s journey is traced from training bases in Southport and Moose Jaw to his role as one of four astronauts aboard Artemis II. “Jeremy’s journey from the training bases of Southport and Moose Jaw to the Artemis II mission underscores the importance of Canada’s military aircrew training, not only for national defence, but for Canada’s continued leadership and contribution on the global stage,” says SkyAlyne. The message also points to the inspirational impact of Hansen’s path for current trainees in Manitoba and Saskatchewan. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/pol-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1647631135332-bbm2'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1647631135332]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'southport-training-roots-highlighted-as-canadian-astronaut-embarks-on-artemis-ii-mission'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); “Jeremy’s journey is also a poignant reminder for other students at Southport and Moose Jaw: even the sky isn’t the limit.” Hansen, a colonel in the Royal Canadian Air Force, was selected by the Canadian Space Agency in 2009 and made history in 2023 when he was named to the Artemis II crew, becoming the first Canadian astronaut assigned to a mission beyond low Earth orbit. As the mission continues, SkyAlyne expressed support for Hansen and his fellow crew members. “As Artemis II prepares for launch, SkyAlyne is proud to wish Col. Jeremy Hansen and his crewmates a safe and successful mission. Beginning at home in Canada, his story reflects the very best of the Royal Canadian Air Force and the training systems that support it.” “Blue skies and tailwinds, Jeremy — Sic Itur Ad Astra. We can’t wait to see you with your astronaut wings!” googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/pol-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1647631135332-bbm3'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1647631135332]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'southport-training-roots-highlighted-as-canadian-astronaut-embarks-on-artemis-ii-mission'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); Hansen’s journey from Canadian training facilities to deep space continues to resonate locally, with Southport Aerospace Centre serving as an early stepping stone in a career now making history on the global stage.

science.title: How AI Is Advancing Cryo-Electron Tomography
science.description: HHMI Investigators are using AI to push cryo-electron tomography beyond cellular imaging to the molecular level — revealing how DNA, synapses, and other structures are organized inside cells.

science.title: The depths of Neptune and Uranus may be 'superionic'
science.description: The interiors of ice giant planets like Uranus and Neptune could be home to a previously unknown state of matter, according to new computational simulations by Carnegie's Cong Liu and Ronald Cohen. Their work, published in Nature Communications, predicts that a quasi-one-dimensional superionic state of carbon hydride exists under the extreme pressures and temperatures found deep inside these outer solar system bodies.

science.title: How did this get made? Giant planet orbits small star
science.description: New observations of "forbidden" planet TOI5205-b reveal surprising details about it its atmospheric chemistry.

science.title: Rubin Observatory Spots Over 11,000 Asteroids in Record Haul

science.title: New genetic toolkit enables genome-wide analysis
science.description: Researchers at Cornell University have developed a powerful new genetic toolkit that allows scientists to study how genes function at the level of individual cells, an advance that could accelerate discoveries in development, neuroscience and disease.

science.title: Engineering the bite of ancient marine predators
science.description: An international team of researchers, led by paleontologists of the University of Liège, has investigated the biting capabilities of extinct predatory marine reptiles, revealing how these formidable predators could coexist within the same ecosystem. This work sheds new light on the hunting strategies of long-extinct predators that dominated the seas during the Age of Dinosaurs. The research is published in the journal Palaeontology.
